content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

The amendment identifies a real bead-ID consistency bug. The controller stores canonicalized milestone-qualified IDs on selection, but several later paths still compare bead IDs as plain strings, so explicit short bead IDs can diverge between controller state and task/project lineage and trigger false corruption/alignment failures.

## Votes

- `fr-9-3f12f18c`: Accept
  `select_next_milestone_bead` canonicalizes an unqualified ready bead like `bead-x` into `ms-id.bead-x` before persisting it as `active_bead_id`, but later execution calls such as `sync_controller_task_claimed` pass `task_source.bead_id` through to `validate_active_context_alignment`, which still does a raw `current_bead_id != bead_id` check. The codebase also preserves short explicit bead IDs in milestone/task lineage paths, so this is not just hypothetical. That means a controller can legitimately hold `ms-id.bead-x` while the task source still carries `bead-x`, and the next start/resume/sync will fail with the cited "cannot adopt" error even though both identifiers refer to the same bead. The amendment is technically correct; the safe fix is to make downstream comparisons normalization-aware everywhere this alignment is checked.
